name: Step-3
archDisplay: MoE — 48 experts (3 routed + 1 shared per token), Multi-Matrix Factorization Attention (MFA) + Attention-FFN Disaggregation (AFD)
note: Open-source 321B MoE (38B active) multimodal reasoning VLM, Apache-2.0, co-designed with MFA/AFD for cost-effective decoding.
tpsNote: 321B total params must stay resident even with 38B active — infeasible on a single consumer GPU; needs multi-GPU/datacenter serving.
verificationNotes: Confirmed real and independently corroborated: 321B total/38B active MoE, Apache-2.0 code+weights, and July 31, 2025 release date all confirmed via HuggingFace (stepfun-ai/step3), GitHub (stepfun-ai/Step3), and the arXiv system paper (2507.19427, submitted 2025-07-25). Context length 64K (65536 tokens) confirmed via HF model card text. Pricing $0.57/$1.42 per 1M input/output tokens confirmed via independent pricing aggregator search. DROPPED 4 of 5 result rows (gpqa 67.1, aime 73, livecodebench 83.7, math-vision 64.2): the official capability-benchmark table on stepfun's research page exists only as an embedded image (figures/step3_bmk.jpeg per the GitHub README, not machine-readable text), and repeated independent fetch attempts of that same page/image produced mutually contradictory numbers for GPQA-Diamond (read as both 70.0 and 73.0), AIME25 (read as both 67.1 and 82.9), and LiveCodeBench (read as both 83.7 and 67.1) -- none could be literally confirmed with confidence, so per instructions they were dropped. Kept only MMMU=74.2, which was consistently and independently reproduced across three separate fetches/searches (direct page fetch, a third-party SiliconFlow blog post in narrative text, and general web search synthesis).
